rationale: Both candidates converge on the same correct diagnosis (PR #4527 regression fixed by #4792, already folded into the 0.24 rebase) and both quote the exact ground-truth code comment verbatim, so correctness is essentially tied. X reads as a cleaner, more maintainer-voiced reply, proactively addresses potential red herrings in the reporter's environment (NVFP4 patch line, timeout-value differences across versions) and gives layered remediation options (upgrade / cherry-pick / workaround). Y is equally grounded in the core fix but is formatted as an internal 'Run report' with a raw task JSON header rather than a direct reply, and adds a couple of unverifiable specifics (an invented pytest test name, NPU runner claims) beyond what the thread shows, slightly weakening its grounding relative to X.
